item.title: The Greenville Eight and Library Discrimination, Then and Now: Book Censorship News, March 6, 2026
item.description: The Greenville Eight protested library discrimination in 1960. Their fight and others like them are a legacy that continues to play out now.

item.title: 4 Buzzy Books Your Patrons Are Hearing About
item.description: These four books–all by women authors–are generating a lot of buzz. Here's what to know and why your patrons will be asking for them.

item.title: New Horror Books to Haunt and Horrify You in March 2026
item.description: March's slate of new horror releases is scary good. Here's what you'll want on your TBR.

item.title: Graphic Novels About Losing a Loved One
item.description: No one wants to experience loss, but it will happen to all of us. These graphic novels grapple with losing a loved one in meaningful ways.

item.title: Where Past Meets Present: Must-Read Modern Classics of Historical Fiction
item.description: Where should you begin when it comes to reading historical fiction that stands the test of time? We've got several ideas!
